subject: Civics
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse the concept and importance of public facilities
Examine the role of government in providing essential services
Evaluate the importance of clean drinking water as a public facility
Understand the differences between public and private services
Analyse inequality in access to public facilities
Evaluate the role of citizens and government in ensuring equal access

lesson_plan.title: Concept and Importance of Public Facilities
topics_covered: Examples such as water supply, sanitation and transport
Importance of these services in society
student_activities: Analyse how public services affect daily life
Write examples of essential public facilities

lesson_plan.title: Clean Drinking Water as a Public Facility
student_activities: Discuss problems caused by lack of clean water
Explain why water supply should be a public responsibility

lesson_plan.title: Role of Government in Public Facilities
topics_covered: Use of taxes to fund services
student_activities: Discuss how governments provide services like hospitals and roads
List examples of services funded by taxes

lesson_plan.title: Public and Private Services
topics_covered: Differences between public and private services
Advantages and limitations of private services
student_activities: Compare government hospitals and private hospitals
Discuss why some services should remain public

lesson_plan.title: Inequality in Access to Public Facilities
student_activities: Analyse examples where facilities are not equally available

lesson_plan.title: Citizen Responsibility and Chapter Review
topics_covered: Role of citizens in protecting public facilities
student_activities: Write a short reflection on responsible use of public services
Participate in a quiz covering the chapter

teacher_tip: Encourage students to examine local examples of public facilities and analyse how equal access to services can improve social welfare and development.
